briefdescription.para: Flag to enable or disable PyAMI (ATLAS Metadata Interface) usage. Default true.
Path to the directory for caching metadata. Default current directory.
Current working directory path. Default ".".
Name for Sum-Of-Weights information, if applicable. Default empty.
Vector to store names of TTrees to be processed. Initialized empty.
Vector to store names of TBranches to be processed. Initialized empty.
Vector to store names of TLeaves (variables) to be processed. Initialized empty.
Map storing pointers to open TFile objects, keyed by filename. Initialized empty.
Map storing pointers to meta objects (metadata), keyed by an identifier (e.g., filename or sample label). Initialized empty.
Nested map storing TTree pointers: Filename -> (Tree Name -> TTree*). Initialized empty.
Nested map storing TTree entry counts: Filename -> (Tree Name -> N_Entries). Initialized empty.
Nested map storing TBranch pointers: Branch Path (e.g., File/Tree) -> (Branch Name -> TBranch*). Initialized empty.
Nested map storing TLeaf pointers: Filename -> (Leaf Name -> TLeaf*). Initialized empty.
Nested map storing TLeaf type names: Filename -> (Leaf Name -> Type String). Initialized empty.
Map indicating if a given string (filename) corresponds to a (successfully opened) ROOT file. Initialized empty.

memberdef.type: std::map< std::string, std::map< std::string, std::map< std::string, std::vector< std::string > > > >
memberdef.name: keys

briefdescription.para: Complex nested map for storing scanned keys from ROOT files (e.g., File -> Tree -> Branch -> Leaves).
Name of the currently open file. Initialized empty.
Current reader mode ("read" or "write"). Initialized empty.
Filename associated with data, possibly for metadata. Initialized empty.
Filename associated with metadata. Initialized empty.
Maps a file identifier to its access path. Initialized empty.
Maps directory paths to a list of files within them. Initialized empty.
Cache for directory listings. Initialized empty.
Map storing data read from files, keyed by an identifier. Initialized empty.
Map storing TTreeReader objects, keyed by an identifier (e.g., tree name). Initialized empty.
Pointer to an HDF5 file object. Initialized to nullptr.
Pointer to a custom hdf5 handler class instance. Initialized to nullptr.
Pointer to the settings object. Initialized to nullptr.
Flag indicating if in write mode. Initialized to false.
Flag related to HDF5 event processing mode. Initialized to true.
Flag indicating if HDF5 is being used. Initialized to false.
Flag indicating if ROOT is being used. Initialized to false.
< Public access specifier for the following members.

detaileddescription.para: Constructor for the io class. Initializes a new io instance.
Initializes the io object with the prefix "io".

briefdescription.para: Destructor for the io class.

detaileddescription.para: Cleans up resources, such as closing open files.
Cleans up resources by closing open files and freeing memory.

briefdescription.para: Templated method to write a vector of objects to a dataset.

parameterdescription.para: The type of objects in the vector.
Pointer to a vector of objects of type g to be written.
The name of the dataset to write to.

inbodydescription.para: < Get the type name of g.
< Write data to HDF5.

briefdescription.para: Templated method to write a single object to a dataset.

parameterdescription.para: The type of the object.
Pointer to an object of type g to be written.
The name of the dataset to write to.

inbodydescription.para: < Get the type name of g.
< Write data to HDF5.

briefdescription.para: Templated method to read a vector of objects from a dataset.

parameterdescription.para: The type of objects in the vector.
Pointer to a vector of objects of type g where read data will be stored.
The name of the dataset to read from.

inbodydescription.para: < Read data from HDF5.

briefdescription.para: Templated method to read a single object from a dataset.

parameterdescription.para: The type of the object.
Pointer to an object of type g where read data will be stored.
The name of the dataset to read from.

inbodydescription.para: < Read data from HDF5.

briefdescription.para: Reads graph data in HDF5 format specifically for graph_hdf5_w struct.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a graph_hdf5_w object to store the read data.
The name of the dataset to read from.

briefdescription.para: Starts an I/O session, opening a file for reading or writing.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the file to open.
Mode of operation: "read" or "write".

simplesect.para: True if the file was successfully opened in the specified mode, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Ends the current I/O session, closing any open files.
Retrieves the names of datasets available in the currently open HDF5 file.

simplesect.para: A vector of strings, where each string is a dataset name.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the sizes (number of entries) of TTrees in the open ROOT files.

simplesect.para: A map where keys are TTree names (or unique identifiers) and values are their sizes (long).

briefdescription.para: Checks the validity or accessibility of ROOT file paths defined in settings or configurations.
Scans for keys (e.g., TTree names, TBranch names) in the open ROOT files.

simplesect.para: True if keys were successfully scanned, false otherwise (e.g., no files open).

briefdescription.para: Initializes ROOT file processing, potentially opening files specified in settings.
Finalizes ROOT file processing, closing any ROOT files opened by root_begin.
Triggers the generation of a ROOT PCM (Precompiled Module) if needed for dictionary generation.
Imports settings from a settings_t object to configure the io instance.

detaileddescription.para: Imports settings into the IO module.

params
Pointer to a settings_t object.
This function configures the IO module based on the provided settings. It sets up the metacache path, sum of weights name, and other parameters.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the settings structure.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the data read from ROOT files, likely structured as a map of data_t objects.

simplesect.para: Pointer to a map where keys are data identifiers (e.g., branch names) and values are data_t pointers.

briefdescription.para: Splits a string into a vector of substrings based on a delimiter.

parameterdescription.para: The input string to split.
The delimiter.

simplesect.para: A vector of substrings.

briefdescription.para: Removes whitespace from the beginning and end of a string.

parameterdescription.para: The string to trim.

simplesect.para: The trimmed string.

briefdescription.para: Gets the type name of a variable as a string (for debugging).

parameterdescription.para: The type to get the name of.

simplesect.para: The name of the type as a string.

inbodydescription.para: < Gets the type name via RTTI.
< Returns the type name.

briefdescription.para: Sums the elements of a vector.

parameterdescription.para: The type of elements in the vector.
Pointer to the vector to sum.

simplesect.para: The sum of all elements.

inbodydescription.para: < Initialize the output variable.
< Return the sum.

briefdescription.para: Sets the prefix used in log messages from this instance.

parameterdescription.para: The string to use as a prefix for log messages.

briefdescription.para: Returns the prefix currently used in log messages.

simplesect.para: The current prefix string.

briefdescription.para: Outputs an info message.

parameterdescription.para: The message to output.
Whether to append a newline (default: true).

briefdescription.para: Outputs a warning message.

parameterdescription.para: The warning message to output.
Whether to append a newline (default: true).

briefdescription.para: Outputs an error message.

parameterdescription.para: The error message to output.
Whether to append a newline (default: true).

briefdescription.para: Outputs a debug message (only in debug mode).

parameterdescription.para: The debug message to output.
Whether to append a newline (default: true).

briefdescription.para: Outputs a message with custom formatting.

parameterdescription.para: The message to output.
A custom tag to prepend to the message.
Whether to append a newline (default: true).

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables debug mode.

parameterdescription.para: Boolean flag, set to true to enable debug messages, false to disable.

briefdescription.para: Returns the current status of debug mode.

simplesect.para: Boolean indicating whether debug mode is enabled (true) or disabled (false).

briefdescription.para: Sets whether warnings should be suppressed.

parameterdescription.para: Boolean flag, set to true to suppress warnings, false to show them.

briefdescription.para: Returns the current warning suppression status.

simplesect.para: Boolean indicating whether warnings are suppressed (true) or shown (false).

briefdescription.para: Sets whether info messages should be suppressed.

parameterdescription.para: Boolean flag, set to true to suppress info messages, false to show them.

briefdescription.para: Returns the current info message suppression status.

simplesect.para: Boolean indicating whether info messages are suppressed (true) or shown (false).

briefdescription.para: Creates a directory path, including parent directories if needed.

parameterdescription.para: The directory path to create.

briefdescription.para: Deletes a directory path and its contents.

parameterdescription.para: The directory path to delete.

briefdescription.para: Checks if a path refers to an existing file.

parameterdescription.para: The path to check.

simplesect.para: True if the path is a file and exists, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Renames a file or directory.

parameterdescription.para: The original path.
The new path.

briefdescription.para: Converts a relative path to an absolute path.

parameterdescription.para: The path to convert.

simplesect.para: The absolute version of the path.

briefdescription.para: Lists files in a directory, optionally filtered by extension.

parameterdescription.para: The directory to search.
File extension filter. If empty, all files are listed.

simplesect.para: A vector of filenames in the directory.

briefdescription.para: Converts a double value to a string with standard precision.

parameterdescription.para: The double value to convert.

simplesect.para: The string representation of the value.

briefdescription.para: Converts a double value to a string with specified precision.

parameterdescription.para: The double value to convert.
The number of decimal places to consider.

simplesect.para: The string representation of the value.

briefdescription.para: Replaces all occurrences of a substring in a string.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the string to modify.
The substring to replace.
The replacement string.

briefdescription.para: Checks if a string contains a specific substring.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the string to check.
The substring to search for.

simplesect.para: True if the substring is found, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Checks if a string ends with a specific substring.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the string to check.
The substring to check for at the end.

simplesect.para: True if the string ends with the substring, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Checks if a vector of strings contains a specific string.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the vector to search.
The string to search for.

simplesect.para: True if the string is found in the vector, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Empties a vector and all objects it points to.

parameterdescription.para: The type of pointers in the vector.
Pointer to the vector to empty.

inbodydescription.para: < Loop through all elements in the vector.
< Delete the object the pointer points to.
< Set the pointer to nullptr to avoid dangling pointers.
< Clear the vector.

briefdescription.para: Fills a vector with pointers from another vector based on a selection index.

parameterdescription.para: The type of objects being pointed to.
Pointer to the output vector to fill.
Pointer to the source vector to select from.
Pointer to a vector of indices for selection.

inbodydescription.para: < Clear the output vector.
< Reserve space for the output elements.
< Loop through each selected index.
< Get the object at the selected index.
< Add it to the output vector.
< Mark the object as in use.

briefdescription.para: Extracts a specific property from a vector of objects into a new vector.

parameterdescription.para: The type of objects in the source vector.
The type of the property to extract.
Pointer to the output vector to fill with property values.
Pointer to the source vector of objects.
Function pointer to extract the property from an object.

inbodydescription.para: < Clear the properties vector.
< Reserve space for the property values.
< Loop through each object in the source vector.
< Create a default-initialized property variable.
< Call the provided function to extract the property.
< Add the property value to the output vector.

briefdescription.para: Prefix used in log messages from this instance. Initialized as empty string.
Flag indicating whether debug mode is enabled. Initialized as false.
Flag indicating whether warnings are suppressed. Initialized as false.
Flag indicating whether info messages are suppressed. Initialized as false.
< Inherits from the notification class for logging capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: Manages input and output operations for analysis data.

The io class provides an interface for reading from and writing to various data formats, with a focus on ROOT files (TTrees) and HDF5 files. It handles file opening/closing, data reading/writing for different data types (single objects, vectors of objects), and manages internal data structures for tracking file and dataset information.

node.label: std::map< std::string, std::map< std::string, std::map< std::string, std::vector< std::string > > > >

childnode.edgelabel: < std::string, std::map< std::string, std::map< std::string, std::vector< std::string > > > >

node.label: map< std::string, std::map< std::string, std::vector< std::string > > >
